preview_text:
The model favours Turkey with a 38% win probability, leaving the USA at 31% and a draw at 31%. This is a competitive fixture in which no outcome holds a commanding edge; the three-percentage-point gap between favourite and underdog reflects relatively balanced underlying strength.

The draw emerges as a genuine possibility at near one-in-three odds, suggesting the match is likely to be tightly contested. The spread across all three outcomes is narrow, indicating a fixture where either side has plausible paths to victory and a stalemate is far from unlikely. Scorelines that favour neither team are as probable as a USA win.

The model and sportsbook consensus diverge notably. The market prices a USA win at 38%, some 7 percentage points higher than the model, and correspondingly underweights both Turkey (down 3pp) and the draw (down 5pp). Polymarket prices align more closely with the sportsbooks than with the model on this matchup. The dashboard does not adjudicate between these sources; the divergence suggests material disagreement about the balance of play.
